narrative.description: WipEout (1995) is a acclaimed racing title recognized in retro gaming lists for its distinctive mechanics, pacing, and lasting influence on the genre.
narrative.core_loop: Master tracks, tune vehicles, and beat opponents to the finish line.

mechanics.map_notes: Core momentum based speed loop defines WipEout's identity.
mechanics.expression: Character velocity accumulates from slopes, boosts, and sustained input, making speed a core traversal resource.

mechanics.map_notes: Core license progression gates loop defines WipEout's identity.
mechanics.expression: Structured license tests or certifications unlock higher vehicle tiers and race categories.

mechanics.map_notes: Core vehicle tuning economy loop defines WipEout's identity.
mechanics.expression: Currency earned from races funds parts and tuning upgrades that modify vehicle performance.

mechanics.map_notes: Mission Select Structure supports the main loop in WipEout.
mechanics.expression: A menu or hub lets the player choose missions or levels in flexible order with difficulty ratings.

mechanics.map_notes: Optional Content Layering supports the main loop in WipEout.
mechanics.expression: Hidden rooms, passages, or content zones exist off main paths, requiring players to investigate suspicious environments.

mechanics.map_notes: Depletable Health Pool supports the main loop in WipEout.
mechanics.expression: A survival mechanic where the player character possesses a finite quantity of health points that decrease when taking damage, with depletion resulting in failure.

mechanics.map_notes: Telegraphed Threat Loop supports the main loop in WipEout.
mechanics.expression: A combat encounter structure in which an enemy signals an incoming attack through a distinct visual or audio cue before the attack resolves.

mechanics.map_notes: Environmental Hazard Navigation supports the main loop in WipEout.
mechanics.expression: A traversal mechanic in which the level space contains persistent damaging or lethal obstacles that the player must avoid through precise movement.

mechanics.map_notes: Combo Trick Scoring supports the main loop in WipEout.
mechanics.expression: Chained tricks and aerial maneuvers multiply score within a combo timer during skating or stunt gameplay.

mechanics.map_notes: Life Based Retry System supports the main loop in WipEout.
mechanics.expression: A failure-buffer system in which the player is granted a limited number of retries, losing one life upon failure and restarting from a previous checkpoint.

mechanics.map_notes: Mid Level Checkpoint supports the main loop in WipEout.
mechanics.expression: A save point within a stage resets the player to that position on death rather than restarting the entire level.

mechanics.map_notes: Input Aligned Movement supports the main loop in WipEout.
mechanics.expression: Movement responds directly and immediately to input direction with minimal inertia, optimized for top-down or free-aim combat spaces.

mechanics.map_notes: Loop Geometry Traversal supports the main loop in WipEout.
mechanics.expression: Level geometry includes curved loops and tunnels that the player traverses when carrying sufficient momentum.

mechanics.map_notes: Linear Stage Gauntlet supports the main loop in WipEout.
mechanics.expression: Progression moves through a fixed sequence of confined stages without hub backtracking.

mechanics.map_notes: Purchase Gated Upgrade supports the main loop in WipEout.
mechanics.expression: Players collect currency through gameplay and exchange it at vendors for permanent upgrades, creating economic progression choices.

design_intent.player_fantasy: A racing experience centered on WipEout.
